description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single build stage and a job named build_modpacks that uses the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/kirara/launcher/builder and runs only on the master branch. The job runs manually, sets variables CONTAINER_NAME as ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-web, IMAGE_NAME as ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/web, and PORT as '8080', and executes the following shell commands in order: git lfs pull; ./build_packs.py; for each directory in server it echoes "Packing modpack $modpack", creates a tar.gz archive with tar -czvf server/${modpack}.tar.gz -C server/${modpack} . and removes the original directory; docker build -f Dockerfile.web -t ${IMAGE_NAME} .; docker stop ${CONTAINER_NAME} && docker rm ${CONTAINER_NAME} || true; and docker run -d --name ${CONTAINER_NAME} -p "$PORT:80" ${IMAGE_NAME}. The pipeline sets GIT_STRATEGY to clone and tags the runner with docker-host.
